claims.subject: T-market-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We see a normalization of the volume growth because we balance – we have it more – a better pricing and we also see that activity levels should not be quite as high as what we experienced in terms of demand in Q1.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL anticipates a normalization of volume growth and lower activity levels compared to Q1.
claims.subject: T-market-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We should be able to deliver around 13%.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL aims to deliver an EBIT margin of around 13%.
claims.subject: T-ebit-margin-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We opted to [ph] 20%, 25% (00:20:20), which means it's a bit higher, but it's a combination of the price and what we have seen in volumes and certain assumptions on second half year.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects a top line growth of 20% to 25% for the second half of the year, driven by a combination of price and volume.
claims.subject: T-fy2021-financial-guidance

claims.speaker: Kim Junge Andersen
claims.quote: We will see a CapEx amount going up in the coming years.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects an increase in CapEx in the coming years.
claims.subject: T-capex-guidance

claims.speaker: Kim Junge Andersen
claims.quote: We have said it'll go up to a CapEx ratio of up to 13%.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL has set a target for CapEx to reach a ratio of up to 13%.
claims.subject: T-capex-guidance

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We expect that some sort of reaction will come.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL anticipates a reaction in the market due to high material prices and rising interest rates.
claims.subject: T-market-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We believe in a good demand growth for our products and that means that we like to sit on about 13% EBIT margin or a bit above that because we need to invest a lot to build capacity.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL expects good demand growth for its products and aims to maintain an EBIT margin of about 13% or higher to support capacity investments.
claims.subject: T-ebit-margin-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We expect to see a decline in Russia.
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL predicts a decline in its business in Russia.
claims.subject: T-market-outlook

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We can grow definitely more than 3%, right?
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL believes it can achieve growth of more than 3% in the future.
claims.subject: T-capacity-for-growth

claims.speaker: Jens Birgersson
claims.quote: We think a single-digit growth of the absolute number of stone wool will be delivered. That – that's probably – that's what we forecast for the year, okay?
claims.paraphrase: ROCKWOOL forecasts single-digit growth in the absolute number of stone wool delivered for the year.
claims.subject: T-capacity-for-growth
